title: HDLBits: the LeetCode for hardware — except the bugs don't just fail tests, they become circuits
body: The analogy holds better than I expected. HDLBits is structured problem-solving with immediate feedback, exactly like algorithmic practice platforms. The difference is what you're building.

Software bugs fail tests. Hardware bugs become circuits. A wrong always block doesn't throw an exception — it synthesizes into logic that quietly does the wrong thing, and you might not find out until a waveform tells you three hours later.

Day one was wires, nets, and vectors. The interesting part wasn't getting the problems accepted. It was tracing signal propagation: following a value through the logic and asking why a particular output exists at a particular time.
I spent more time reading the circuit than writing code. That felt like the right instinct.
Dataflow modelling for now. The plan maps out from here.
